description: After an argument, someone turns the exchange over and notices what they missed. With 'themio', they return in thought to something already experienced or considered. 'romishe' (contemplate) may linger over any subject; reflection begins with something already lived or held in mind.
articulatory_notes: Rest the tongue tip against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th' and let the air continue into true-mid front unrounded 'e'. Close the lips for voiced 'm', open the nasal passage, and release into stressed high-front unrounded 'i'. Draw the tongue back and round the lips for final true-mid back 'o', and keep it separate across the 'i.o' hiatus rather than inserting a glide.
usage_notes: A stated object identifies the experience or subject under reflection; context may supply it instead. Imperfective 'si' presents reflection as still underway. Noun 'themio' means reflection or one reflective episode.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Reflection returns to an experience after the first reaction has loosened. That distance can expose a habit that was hard to see while it was happening.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: After a difficult exchange, 'themio' returns a speaker to what they said and what followed before they speak again.

semantic_domains.cognition: returning in thought to experience or material already considered
semantic_domains.wisdom: reconsideration of experience before judgement settles
